programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan tries to find meaning in a murdered private eye's last words, while Jack and Steve vie for the attentions of the dead man's secretary. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part one of two. Dr Sloan faces an agonising dilemma while investigating the murder of an eminent plastic surgeon and ends up helping to charge the man's widow

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Working at a small-town market, Kimble is held up by a fellow employee who also happens to be the local sheriff's son

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: An artist plans to fake his own death to boost the value of his work, but the plot backfires when he is found murdered

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A wrecked plane leads the lawman to investigate a robbery and murder that took place eight years earlier

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella investigate a new fantasy game sweeping New York, and discover that harmless role-playing has begun to overlap with reality in deadly fashion. Meanwhile, a woman's death is traced to an exotic cuisine event where the dishes include live centipedes, Brazilian cockroaches, fried tarantulas and other dubious delicacies

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Marine is seemingly killed in a mortar attack on America's heavily fortified administrative HQ in Baghdad, but a subsequent autopsy reveals he was murdered. Intelligence analyst Nikki Jardine volunteers to help Tony investigate the case - but her behaviour in Iraq makes him suspect she had an ulterior motive for requesting the assignment

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sam, Callen and the rest of the team make their way through Frank's list, tracking down the remaining sleeper agents hiding in America's seven largest cities. But when raids recover no nuclear bombs, they draw a blank - until a Russian arms dealer makes it known he has three nukes for sale on the black market. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Richard Kimble works as a crewman on a yacht chartered by an embezzler's wife who is under police surveillance

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry Mason investigates when a race car driver is murdered after deliberately wrecking a valuable test car

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Inquiries into the death of a marine land the team members squarely in the middle of an inner-city gang war - and a message found at the scene of the crime seemingly implicates Gibbs. Forced to confront a secret from his past, he calls his mentor Mike Franks for help. Drama, guest starring Muse Watson and Kari Coleman, with Mark Harmon

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Alex, CD and a group of children are taken hostage, and find themselves in the path of a dangerous cyclone

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. A military aircraft transporting the bodies of dead US marines back home crashes before reaching its destination, and Gibbs and the team are called in to help identify human remains at the site of the wreckage. However, a DNA discrepancy turns a routine investigation into a search for a missing person. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A naval technology scientist is being rushed to hospital with a gunshot wound, only for an unknown assailant to stop the ambulance and finish the job there and then, while also killing everyone else on board. The investigation takes an intriguing turn when the post-mortem reveals an object was removed from the victim's chest, and even more so when an unlikely link emerges between the dead man and a surfer, giving Deeks and Kensi the chance to pull on their wetsuits and head down to the beach. Eric Christian Olsen and Daniela Ruah star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: One of Tony's old murder investigations is reopened and, in an attempt to find the perpetrator, he decides to switch roles with Gibbs, leading the team in an inquiry that links the case to an embezzlement scheme. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Sasha Alexander, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.desc: Part two of two. Ziva and Gibbs lead the mission to rescue the captured Marine in Afghanistan before discovering that her captor's brothers have bigger plans and are at large in America. Gibbs has to be stopped when he tries to employ methods similar to those used on Kinah and Lala in an effort to gain information, but Vance has a trick that gives the agents an idea of what the siblings have in mind

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A millionaire former CIA man meets a premature end when someone plants a controlled explosive in his lighter. Over the years he had built up an impressive list of enemies, so finding his killer looks like an uphill task, but as the agents start working through the potential culprits, a recovered computer file leads them to an escort service used by a wanted hitman. However, what they don't bank on is the chief suspect turning up dead

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Chris's unexpected weekend leave from prison infuriates Tracey, while his model behaviour arouses wife Sharon's suspicions. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson, Lesley Joseph and Peter Polycarpou

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: The success of a new business venture causes Sharon and Tracey's lives to take a new direction. Meanwhile, Dorien advises a depressed Garth, who's desperate to find out what life's all about. With Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Suspected rapist Tom Norris stands trial but walks away from court a free man after the victim's plucky effort to give evidence crumbles under tough interrogation from Banksy's wife Naomi Woods. Sam is obsessed with nailing her suspect and picks over the case in minute detail, but forensic evidence offers no help. When a similar crime is committed at the same scene, it is Banksy who realises they must have been looking in the wrong place

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Lulu is frustrated when Sacha prevents her from assisting Michael in surgery, and she deliberately places a patient in jeopardy in an effort to undermine him. Meanwhile, Chantelle unwittingly puts Elizabeth in grave danger while trying to repair her relationship with Simone, and Sahira worries about Greg as he tries to forget his past by flirting endlessly with the hospital's female staff

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part Christmas special from 2006, tensions run high as staff deal with an influx of emergencies on one of the busiest days of the year, and Ellen's nightmare continues as she undergoes an MRI scan to find out whether her cancer has returned. Laura brings in her daughter Rosie, complaining of stomach pains, but when the pair's story fails to add up it becomes apparent all is not what it seems. A dejected Josh comes under fire from Dixie as he puts his life at risk to save a woman trapped in a frozen pond. However, as events spiral out of control back at the hospital, he finds himself staring death in the face for a second time

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sam struggles to get a lead on Tom Norris in the rape case and new information from Naomi throws the spotlight on his housemate Dan Cox, who once covered for his friend to help him escape sexual harassment charges. The theory that the pair are working together to prey on young women seems more plausible - until Norris turns up bound and beaten in the boot of Cox's car

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Lenny is woken by an angry George. Lorraine arrives back in the Square with Joe and goes to the Vic to see Grant. Martin is beaten up by Shane's gang

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Martin's involvement with the gang escalates as they plan a heist of the community centre

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: The medic temporarily hangs up his stethoscope to intervene in a confrontation between Irish farm labourers and irate locals who blame them for a typhus outbreak, resolving matters with a combination of tough talking and common sense. David Rintoul stars, with Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: A retired sheepdog takes to rounding up cars, and James is challenged to a race. Rural drama based on the novels of James Herriot, starring Christopher Timothy, Peter Davison and Robert Hardy

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Lady Jane makes a spectacular reappearance in Lovejoy's life just as he decides it is time to settle down - leaving him forced to choose between her and Charlotte. Meanwhile, faithful companion Tinker prepares to retire just as Eric Catchpole makes an unscheduled return to the fold. Last ever episode of the comedy drama, starring Ian McShane, Dudley Sutton, Caroline Langrishe, Phyllis Logan and Chris Jury

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: Hester lends a hand with a coach tour for British buyers of French homes, while William has plumbing worries. The pair are settling in well, but the good life turns sour when their daughter visits. Comedy, starring Julia Mackenzie and Anton Rodgers

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tests confirm Tom has prostate trouble and Harvey is excited when a representative from Bayview has been chosen to attend the Council of European Old People. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth dreads the idea of attending Onslow's birthday celebrations. However, she changes her mind when she learns a limousine will be arriving to take her to the party. Starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Smiler goes on the run when he has a tipple too much of Auntie's home-made wine, leaving Compo, Truly and Barry to track him down. Long-running comedy set in Yorkshire, from the pen of Roy Clarke. Peter Sallis stars

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detectives' case against a violent criminal is thrown into jeopardy by the murder of a key witness. Determined to secure a conviction, they immerse themselves in the seedy underworld of a seaside town, and as they press for answers, Havers is brought face to face with her childhood memories. Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra's private and professional worlds become intertwined when her mother has a fall that means she needs to go into a care home. Her first choice is revealed to be the scene of a suspicious death a year earlier, leading Jack, Gerry and Brian to go undercover at the home. Drama, guest starring June Whitfield, Sheila Hancock and Gwyneth Strong

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: The team is assigned to protect murderer Susan Kellar, whose recent release from jail has provoked a storm of protest and forced her to go into hiding for her own safety. Unfortunately, they find themselves unable to protect her family from the lethal attentions of a vigilante. Feature-length crime drama, starring James Macpherson

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: In the conclusion of the 2006 Christmas special, the team battles in earnest to save Josh after he is found lying in a pool of blood in the back of an ambulance. Despite the efforts of the staff, the prognosis looks bleak and his life is left hanging in the balance. Ellen discovers Rosie is missing from the hospital, and as the gravity of the situation hits home she tries desperately to track the girl down before it's too late. The extent of Laura's deceit soon becomes apparent, prompting staff to contact her estranged husband Matthew, who comes into Holby to help them. However, Charlie loses his cool when it transpires he was aware of his wife's illness - and the frantic search for the youngster is stepped up

With a cameo appearance by DJ Scott Mills

programme.desc: Barry comes to Marina's rescue after a nasty bike accident - only to spark rumours of marital disharmony. However, Truly is quick to suggest a remedy for the gossip with the help of borrowed lipstick. Comedy, with Bill Owen, Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton

programme.desc: An armoured security van is discovered at the bottom of a lake, and the team makes a link to a disappearance 17 years previously and an unsolved killing, prompting them to reopen the case. Sandra feels she is making progress after finding the murder weapon, but suffers a setback when a key witness is killed. Detective drama, starring Amanda Redman

programme.desc: The murder of a woman thrown from a bridge appears motiveless until the team is led to a microchip factory where her husband works. programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The MUAs enter the world of special effects, as they are tasked with recreating looks from the smash hit crime drama Peaky Blinders - working at a prestigious make-up academy with guest judge, Peaky's Bafta-winning head make-up designer Loz Scathio. This week's Creative Brief is based on the seven deadly sins, and while one artist's gruesome looks bring out sloth, envy and wrath in very imaginative ways, one MUA's literal interpretation of greed falls flat

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: When 22-year-old Bobbie Edwards had to close her gym Colwyn Bay under lockdown, she launched women-only glutes boot camp courses instead and here reveals the secrets of its success. Bridgend-based entrepreneur Scott Magill reveals how he struck lockdown-gold with his doorstep doughnut delivery service

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma has a big mess to take care of on her arrival home, Norman agrees to seek help, and Dylan receives shocking news about Emma's health

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Caleb and Dylan are confronted by a dangerous proposition, and Sheriff Romero decides to confront Norma

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts views a ground-floor flat in London, Martel Maxwell checks out a former commercial unit in Staffordshire and Dion Dublin look at a three-bedroom semi in Birmingham

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: Fraud specialists seek a serial scam artist faking disability to claim thousands of pounds in insurance payouts, and also uncover the most prolific dental fraudster ever. Plus, a preacher turns prisoner when he's caught with his hand in the public purse. Presented by Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Stories of life in the balance, with Nick Knowles. A father-to-be has a horrific accident while mountainbiking with friends. A man braves a fire at home to search for his dogs

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts, as experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell help two teams find items of value in Nottingham. Anita also goes on a personal journey to see the works of Dame Laura Knight, one of her favourite artists

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: A detoxing Jonno turns on Luca, while Ruhma tries to get Sid to unwrap his feelings. A locked car and a dead phone battery push Zara to the limit

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Hannah, John, Helen and Vince vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple looking for a property in Gloucestershire on a budget of £850,000 and visits an ice cream-making family on Rodburgh Common

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Swindon to meet Saima and her children Hamzah, Hamid and Zakir, who are rather embarrassed by their concrete jungle of a garden. Saima wants a zoned garden that gives her an adult area to entertain as well as a chillout space for the youngsters and has a £2,500 budget for the conversion

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Paul Martin explore antique shops in the West Country. At auction, an expensive biscuit tin leaves a foul taste while a cheap oil painting makes an impression

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. With Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A Panorama investigation uncovering new evidence of A.I. being used by police in China to recognise the emotions of detainees, in order to help determine guilt or innocence. China has vowed to become the world's A.I. superpower by 2030, sparking a new arms race with America, and both countries are pouring billions into cutting-edge military tech, including autonomous weapons. A.I. could usher in a golden age, but without urgent regulation, experts warn we could lose control of artificial intelligence, a prospect, they say, that should scare us all

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steven Fletcher tackles an intricate Boulle-work clock that has not ticked for more than 15 years, and soft toy restorers Julie Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch take on a threadbare wheeled elephant. Woodwork specialist William Kirk gets to grips with one of the show's oldest assignments in the form of a 300-year-old Georgian desk that is showing its age

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett hosts as temperatures drop and winter hits the sewing room, with challenges themed around keeping warm and adding sparkle to the coldest months of the year. Patrick and Esme's Pattern Challenge this week is a man's flannel shirt. The trickiest pattern challenge yet, the sewers must create cuffs and collars, challenging both their construction technique and precise sewing skills

programme.title: Scam City: Money, Mayhem and Maseratis
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar investigates the story of a 20-year-old medical student, who went from handing out cash to strangers to being at the centre of an alleged multi-million-pound scam

programme.title: Scam City: Money, Mayhem and Maseratis
programme.desc: Searching for the elusive Gurvin, Mobeen Azhar hopes to find answers for the people who believe they were scammed - revealing a common thread that blows the story wide open

programme.title: Subnormal: A British Scandal
programme.desc: In the 1960s, West Indian migrants to the UK counted on providing better opportunities for their children. However, there are suggestions that before having a chance to develop intellectually, their offspring were labelled as 'stupid, difficult and disruptive' as psychologists such as Hans Eysenck and Arthur Jensen theorised that black people were genetically less intelligent than white. IQ tests were then based on these theories with the odds stacked against them. Featuring first-hand interviews with key participants of the time, this documentary examines what is potentially one of the biggest scandals in the history of British education

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits Staffordshire to investigate a damp and dingy two-bed terrace, before heading to Rochdale, Greater Manchester, where he discovers a neglected detached house with some beautiful period features. Meanwhile, Martel Maxwell goes to Hertfordshire to find out just what can be done with a pair of old garages

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: A locksmith sees a chance to make quick gains when he is put in charge of a London hospital - and pockets nearly half a million pounds by overcharging the hospital for supplies. Elsewhere, an NHS manager dreams up a scam to siphon over £800,000 of NHS money into his own account. Presented by Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: The emergency services attend a baby who has cut his throat on a glass. Plus, a woman is knocked to the ground and trampled after a dog spooks horses. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant guide two teams scouring the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells for bargains to take to auction. Presenter Charlie Ross monitors their progress and also finds out about a weird and wonderful collection of bicycles

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie's team appreciation gift exchange leads to some big decisions, while Ruhma supports Sid on the day of Lewis' wake. Jimmi helps a struggling student to mend his relationship with his strictly vegan ex-girlfriend

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Andy, Stuart, Alice and Charlotte vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to the Scottish Borders with a couple who want their £450,000 budget to buy them an equestrian property in the country. Jules also visits the dramatic ruins of Melrose Abbey, where the heart of Scotland's medieval king Robert the Bruce is believed to be buried

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and Harry and David Rich help Bath resident Mina, who wants to turn her characterless, overgrown lawn into a Persian paradise. They have a budget of £2,700 to create something Mina will be proud of, and which is evocative of her childhood in the Middle East, including colourful and aromatic planting, stunning tiles, fruit trees and an Iranian-inspired water feature

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Paul Martin visit places including Dorset, Devon and Somerset. Along the way, Paul investigates the obscure, Somerset-based Victorian inventor of unmanned powered flight, while Raj finds out about The Bristol Bus Boycott, a 1960s struggle against racial discrimination. Both experts make profitable finds, with a mule-based fairground ride and an exquisite English oak candle box the star lots at auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mila worries about protecting Kioni as Kathy orders Bobby to look after her, while Vinny wonders about his brother's mystery woman but Kheerat refuses to kiss and tell. He later runs into Sharon, who mentions she's free all day but Kheerat isn't keen - until he sees Gray and suddenly changes his mind. Keegan leaves Sonia with some food for thought

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A London-based entrepreneur with an assortment of naturally derived sugar alternatives hopes he can pull off a sweet deal, while a couple face a roasting over their optimistic projections for their vitamin-infused coffee. Multimillionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies are also approached by an inventor who hopes to fix a deal with her portable children's suction mats and consider investing in a business selling innovative eco-cleaning products. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: What Are We Feeding Our Kids?
programme.desc: Across the world childhood obesity rates have risen tenfold in 50 years. In the UK 21 per cent of children are living with obesity when they leave primary school - the highest it's ever been. Ultra-processed food is cheap and convenient, and accounts for the majority of young people's calorie intake - 64 per cent for children and 67 per cent for teenagers. Yet alarmingly, scientists know very little about exactly how it affects youngsters' bodies. Dr Chris van Tulleken investigates the impact of ultra-processed food, as he undergoes a gruelling self-experiment that even shocks the scientists

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic heptathlon champion Denise Lewis, rugby league great Paul Sculthorpe, former England striker Kevin Phillips and British javelin star Goldie Sayers join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross visits the Ardingly International Antiques Fair, with experts Philip Serrell and Roo Irvine. He also visits a business that conserves historical artifacts

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers come up with competing designs for a Bristol couple on a budget of £6500, The couple want to transform their large, disordered and overgrown garden into an urban suntrap reminiscent of their holidays in the Mediterranean

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don plants out his tomatoes and shows viewers what they can feed their plants. Arit Anderson looks into the use of peat in the horticultural industry, and Sue Kent reveals the progress on her allotment

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold presents highlights and analysis from the latest fixtures, including Warrington Wolves v Castleford Tigers at the Halliwell Jones Stadium

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Mike Reid, Selin Kiazim and Tom Kemble continue the battle for a place at the Taste of Summer banquet, celebrating 140 years of the Wimbledon Championships. This time, the three chefs are tasked to create a main course, with each choosing to use aspects of traditional summer barbecues as their inspiration

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Selin Kiazim, Mike Reid and Tom Kemble continue the battle to cook at a Taste of Summer banquet celebrating 140 years of the Wimbledon Championships. As each of them prepare their desserts, they will all be out to impress as only two can make it through to tomorrow's London and South East final

programme.title: Miniature Britain
programme.desc: Biologist George McGavin travels around Britain with a revolutionary new microscope camera to provide a close-up view of the natural world. The film reveals how caterpillars' feet have hooks that anchor them upside down to leaves, butterfly and moth wings are a kaleidoscope of colourful scales that deter predators, and birds' feathers have thousands of hooks that zip together to keep them airborne. The film-makers also show miniature life in cities, such as creatures grazing the moss on pavements and the legions of dust mites scavenging for food in everyday homes

programme.title: Wild UK
programme.desc: Zoologist Lucy Cooke and wildlife cameraman Colin Stafford-Johnson explore the nation's forests, and see how the pine marten in Scotland is helping animals in Wales

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Michael Baggott assess items brought into Newby Hall in North Yorkshire. Mark comes across an interesting Clarice Cliff cake plate and Michael enjoys an ancient Chinese urn. Presenter Paul Martin takes in the splendours of the country house's Adams interiors

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andrea McLean, Adil Ray, Rita Simons and Phil Wang test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his travels through the capital in the heart of London's East End. First, Michael explores Hackney Wick, where he hears about The Daily Worker newspaper's origins, before heading to London's West End to hunt down London's oldest surviving Indian restaurant, Veeraswamy. Here, he tries his hand at making chapatis, under the watchful eye of the restaurant's master chef

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Dig
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts and Dr Yasmin Khan investigate the excavation of a vast Georgian cemetery near Euston Station to make way for the HS2 rail link. They discover what the site reveals about the lives of the people who were buried there, from the founders of Christie's auction house to a small child who was accidentally buried alive

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan are broadcasting live from Wild Ken Hill in Norfolk, as cameras capture the dramas of such birds as avocets and barn owls. Elsewhere, Iolo Williams encounters some red deer at Alladale Wilderness Reserve in Scotland, and Gillian Burke dips into the vast waters of Strangford Lough in Northern Ireland

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Cameras follow medical staff working in the maternity unit at the University Hospital Coventry. In addition to delivering over 5,500 babies every year, the specialised unit also pioneer tests and treatments as they undertake vital research into the causes of miscarriages and stillbirths

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: David Mitchell, Noel Fielding and Holly Walsh join Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme of 'night' in an extended edition of the panel show. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Saved By a Stranger
programme.desc: Identical twin brothers George and Peter who fled Nazi Germany in 1939 search for the family of the man who helped them escape. Plus, Clare, who housed 21 child refugees from wartorn Bosnia, looks for their mystery schoolgirl translator Petra, whose language skills allowed the children to properly communicate and share the horrors they had fled. Presented by Anita Rani

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: More than 25 years ago, Panorama reporter Martin Bashir secured his global scoop interview with Princess Diana. Now a new Panorama team fronted by journalist John Ware has carried out its own four-month investigation, with exclusive interviews and revelations from internal BBC documents. This is the inside story of how Martin Bashir obtained his career-defining interview and how the BBC responded when it discovered he'd faked bank statements and shown them to Diana's brother, Earl Spencer

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A report on what happens when care goes wrong in hospitals, revealing how some NHS trusts seemingly keep critical reports hidden from the regulator and the public. Repeated scandals have shown how transparency is vital to make sure lessons are learned. However, after a year-long investigation uncovering around 100 unpublished independent reports, reporter and GP Faye Kirkland discovers how hospital failings are apparently being buried

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Documentary following how British Muslims mark some of life's biggest moments. Married couple She and Ali look back at their vows and reflect on how their faith has helped them in their marriage. Emine marks the death of her mother with a final prayer, symbolising her mum coming from god, and going back to god

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Roman Catholics Maria and Peter have their fifth child baptised in a traditional Latin service and in Stockton-on-Tees family and friends pay their last respects to June, one of the first women to be ordained as a priest by the Church of the England in 1994

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick visits Tintagel, Cornwall's most mythical place, which was an important trading port with Europe and the birthplace of perhaps Britain's greatest legend - the tale of King Arthur. He also learns how the Methodists were important to Cornwall, and how they would cook a rather unusual bun during feast days, and meets a family on the Roseland Peninsula who are continuing the surprisingly ancient tradition of growing an exotic spice - saffron

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a retired army couple to find a secluded new home in Norfolk, and also learns about the trade in the world's most expensive spice, saffron

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich meet Liz, Geoff and their daughter Lois in Yate, Gloucestershire, where the hope is they can use a £4,500 budget to create a garden with a nautical theme in memory of Liz's father, who was known locally as ''The Pirate'

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A construction worker hopes his easy-assemble lighting system will spark some interest amid millionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies. Two ex-barmen think their nifty premium whisky subscription business will put them in with a shot of a cash investment, while a construction worker pitches his easy-assemble lighting system. An Isle of Man entrepreneur demonstrates her innovative sand removing powder and the Dragons are intrigued by a quirky nose-mounted air filter. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Antiques-buying challenge presented by Paul Martin in which people are shown the tricks of the trade for buying and selling collectibles, before an auction showdown. In the first edition, Don Gayle from Bolton and Clare Whittaker from Rothbury are put to the test in Gloucestershire

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London and South East chefs prepare their entire menus for judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and newcomer Andi Oliver, who are joined by Davis Cup captain Leon Smith. Together, they decide which contestant to send through to represent the region in the national finals

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: South West chefs Dominic Chapman, Tom Brown and Andy Clatworthy go all out to impress with their starters. Returning chef Dominic serves up a complex summer salad, while newcomer Tom worries his elegant dish may fail to impress the veteran judge, and Andy hopes his mushroom-centred dish will secure his place at the banquet

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan are broadcasting live from Wild Ken Hill in Norfolk, as cameras capture the dramas of such birds as avocets and barn owls. Elsewhere, Iolo Williams encounters some red deer at Alladale Wilderness Reserve in Scotland, and Gillian Burke dips into the vast waters of Strangford Lough in Northern Ireland

programme.title: Wild UK
programme.desc: Lucy Cooke and Colin Stafford-Johnson explore the waters surrounding the UK, discovering how the white-tailed sea eagle has made a dramatic return to the coast

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Newby Hall, North Yorkshire. Mark Stacey comes across two unusual tie and stick pins and Claire Rawle enthuses about a large collection of toy cars

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andrea McLean, Adil Ray, Rita Simons and Phil Wang test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his travels through London in Hampstead, where he learns more about the founder of psychoanalysis, Sigmund Freud, who fled the Austrian capital of Vienna during the Second World War and made his way London. Then, Michael pays a visit to the famous Abbey Road Studios, before making his way to Finsbury and touring the innovative and sculptural housing development, Bevin Court

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Dig
programme.desc: Yasmin Khan and Professor Alice Roberts go in search of the lost remains of some of the burial ground's more famous residents. These include Matthew Flinders, an explorer who expanded the British empire's domains to the then-uncharted coast of Southern Australia, and Bill Richmond, a champion boxer who was born in slavery in Colonial America and fought in the American War of Independence

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Gillian Burke follows the fortunes of a harbour seal in hospital in Northern Ireland, while Iolo Williams is on the look out for golden eagles in the Glen. Elsewhere, Michaela Strachan and Chris Packham assess the effects of this year's late spring on our wildlife

programme.title: The Black American Fight for Freedom
programme.desc: Fifty years on from the promise of equality and the Civil Rights Act, this documentary examines moments where America had the chance to become more equal and why that didn't happen. Through interviews with those who were at the forefront of the struggle for a fair system in education, housing and criminal justice, the film reveals the key court cases, Supreme Court rulings and laws that failed to bridge the gap

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Last week saw the return of the comedy show in which three comedians take the stage of the Hammersmith Apollo in west London. The premise is simple. One comic acts as host, warming up the audience before introducing short sets by two other stand-ups. Future editions promise the likes of Ed Byrne, Rob Beckett, Joel Dommett, Henning Wehn, Lucy Porter, but tonight Nish Kumar plays host, before handing over to David O'Doherty and Luisa Omielan

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Cameras follow medical staff working in the maternity unit at the University Hospital Coventry. In addition to delivering over 5,500 babies every year, the specialised unit also pioneer tests and treatments as they undertake vital research into the causes of miscarriages and stillbirths

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox returns with the programme in which famous faces each bring with them their favourite book of all time to discuss with their fellow guests. In the first edition she is joined by Mel Giedroyc, Griff Rhys Jones, Oti Mabuse and Rick Edwards, who reviewing this week's two book club picks - The Vanishing Half by Brit Bennett and The Fine Art of Invisible Detection by Robert Goddard

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste report on a woman suffering from what she felt was a type of Stockholm syndrome, in that she was unable to escape a relationship with a man who took tens of thousands of pounds from her

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter and Mairi Rattray show how to create a bluebell wood and also lay ready-sown turf to create a near-instant carpet of colour. In sunny Joppa, George Anderso has the results of some of his pruning exploits from last year, while Kirsty Wilson is back in her allotment creating a cold frame from scratch using recycled bits and pieces

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the rich and diverse history of West Penwith, the farthest point west in mainland Britain. He visits Lamorna Cove, made famous through song and art, and the Unesco-protected tin and copper mining area of Botallack, where he talks to a mining explorer. The chef also follows an ancient track in search of Cornwall's oldest building and reveals how, as a young lad on the Lizard Peninsula, he fell in love for the first time and tasted his first French fish stew - known as a bouillabaisse

Meanwhile, his competitors, Andy Clatworthy and Dominic Chapman, are looking to score highly with their more luxurious dishes

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: South West chefs Dominic Chapman, Tom Brown and Andy Clatworthy go all out to impress with their main courses, as they compete for a chance to cook at the Taste of Summer banquet. With a refined summer barbecue going up against a traditional Cornish speciality and a historic Wimbledon-related recipe, who will come out on top?

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Gillian Burke follows the fortunes of a harbour seal in hospital in Northern Ireland, while Iolo Williams is on the look out for golden eagles in the Glen. Elsewhere, Michaela Strachan and Chris Packham assess the effects of this year's late spring on our wildlife

programme.title: Wild UK
programme.desc: The wildlife of the mountains, finding out about ravens, the golden eagles of the Scottish hills and asking whether the UK will ever again play host to a long-lost beast - the lynx

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and David Harper assess items brought to Allerton Castle in North Yorkshire, while Paul Martin finds out about Harrogate's connection with Russia's last tsarina

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andrea McLean, Adil Ray, Rita Simons and Phil Wang test their general knowledge skills in trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael tracks the River Thames from east to west. Starting in Dagenham, he finishes this leg of his tour at one of London's most iconic buildings, Battersea Power Station, built during the 1930s by Giles Gilbert Scott

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Dig
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts and Dr Yasmin Khan reveal how Birmingham's Victorian working class residents made their home city one of the most important industrial centres on the planet. They examine the surviving remains of one of the world's oldest railway terminuses, and discover how many of the thousands of skeletons in the cemetery have evidence of bone damage or amputation, as well as signs of the after-effects of industrial pollution. Plus, a look at the true story behind Peaky Blinders

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan bring the latest dramas from the live nest cameras in Norfolk. Iolo Williams heads to Loch Fleet on Scotland's east coast, hoping to catch ospreys and otters out fishing. Gillian Burke checks out the batty nightlife at WWT Castle Espie, and Megan McCubbin joins conservationists shaping the UK of tomorrow

programme.title: The Road to Partition
programme.desc: Part one of two. On June 22, 1921, King George V and Queen Mary arrived in Belfast for the official opening of the first Northern Ireland parliament. Fearful for their lives, they had come to a city scarred by bitter sectarian violence. One hundred years on, this is the story of the dramatic events that led to the partition of Ireland - one that continues to reverberate to the present day

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and series regular Hugh Dennis look back on the last seven days in the company of guests Maisie Adam, Angela Barnes, Rhys James, Robin Morgan and Ahir Shah

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: DJ Iain Lee gets a call on his phone-in show from Mandy, who tells him how she became trapped in a coffin, having been forced into a robbery. Comedy short written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan, with Natalie Cassidy

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories. With Emma Barnett

programme.title: Gods of Snooker
programme.desc: Desperate for success and tempted by the lucrative rewards brought by towing the line, Jimmy White joined Barry Hearn in the hope he could be turned into a champion like his new stablemate Steve Davis. With White as the hot-favourite and with the crowd urging him on against 21-year-old Stephen Hendry, the final of 1990 world championship turned out to be a pivotal moment in British snooker and paved the way for what was to come in the next decade

programme.title: Makeup: A Glamorous History
programme.desc: Makeup artist Lisa Eldridge explores what the beauty looks of three iconic moments in British history reveal about that era, continuing with the Victorians. In Victorian Britain, makeup was frowned upon, and women were supposed to be naturally beautiful, reflecting their inner purity. Throughout the programme, Lisa delves into this obsession with natural beauty and learns about some of the products created to help achieve this, before creating her own 'no makeup' look inspired by Victorian beauty

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: It's children's week in the sewing room and judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young's pattern challenge choice is a classic for any toddler's wardrobe - a romper suit. The transformation task sees the sewers turning an adult wetsuit into a kids' fancy dress costume, before they tackle a children's raincoat, remembering it must be fully waterproof but allow enough movement for jumping in muddy puddles

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: As the chef's Cornish odyssey nears its end, he takes a break in the city of Truro, where he discovers an unusual ghost story and tours the magnificent cathedral. He also meets a family passionate about offal, who prepare Rick a meal of pickled ox tongue and beef heart tacos, and in the county's most northerly parish he tells the story of an eccentric vicar who would retrieve the bodies from nearby shipwrecks and bury them in his church graveyard

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Canals: The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor tells the story of the people who operated the canal boats, carrying fuel and goods around the country. During the Victorian era they gained reputations for criminality, violence and drinking, but was such a reputation deserved? Liz discovers grisly canal crimes, investigates health and welfare on-board working boats, and looks at why canal children were last on the list to be offered safeguards and formal education. She reveals the campaigners who set out to tackle this injustice, including George Smith of Coalville, Leicestershire, and Sister Mary Ward of Stoke Bruerne, Northamptonshire

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an elegant mountain lake, silent and still, reflecting the natural beauty of leafy trees and grasses all around it

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: Jim Hacker is disturbed to discover his department is responsible for supplying surveillance equipment to the government, which is then used to spy on its own ministers. However, a death threat soon changes his view on the situation. Political satire, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Audrey reprimands DeVere for not fulfilling his duties as lord of the manor when he fails to attend church on Sunday, but then forgets to practise what she preaches. Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles star

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Olden Days
programme.desc: The satirist explores the nation's obsession with the past to reveal how and why the British have continually plundered the 'olden days' to make sense of and shape the present. He begins by examining the various spins put on the lives of King Arthur and Alfred the Great through the ages. He finds out how Arthur has been variously depicted as a wild Celtic warlord, a chivalric hero and a piously questing king, while Alfred's character was manipulated to suit the purposes of tricky medieval lawyers, a Tudor archbishop and a Georgian Prince of Wales

programme.title: Extra Life: A Short History of Living Longer
programme.desc: Steven Johnson and David Olusoga explore the remarkable and often unsung work that has led to major medical advances. Alexander Fleming's famous discovery of antibiotics was followed - in the midst of the Second World War - by the rollout of the manufacture of penicillin at scale, so that lives might be saved at a global level. Pioneering rational drug design in the 1980s led to the development of antiviral drugs for HIV and other previously untreatable diseases. The knowledge of how to produce safe, effective drugs now underpins the work to find treatments for new diseases, such as Covid-19

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: As they contain no active ingredient, placebo medicines and pills should not really work, but they are now being shown to be effective in helping treat pain and depression and even alleviating some of the symptoms of Parkinson's disease. This programme explores why they work and how everyone could benefit from them

programme.title: Pain, Pus & Poison: The Search for Modern Medicines
programme.desc: Michael Mosley investigates mankind's earliest attempts to tackle infection and reveals how microbes are used to fight against disease. The programme also examines how scientists, chemists and doctors, from Louis Pasteur to Howard Florey, each contributed to these medical breakthroughs, and there is a look at the eradication of smallpox

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: Dr Adam Rutherford examines the story of Andreas Vesalius, who in 1537 became the most famous anatomist in Europe. He went on to produce the first complete account of the human body and how to dissect it, and his drawings set the standard for anatomical art for centuries to come, earning him the title of 'the founder of modern anatomy'

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an elegant mountain lake, silent and still, reflecting the natural beauty of leafy trees and grasses all around it

programme.title: Canals: The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor tells the story of the people who operated the canal boats, carrying fuel and goods around the country. During the Victorian era they gained reputations for criminality, violence and drinking, but was such a reputation deserved? Liz discovers grisly canal crimes, investigates health and welfare on-board working boats, and looks at why canal children were last on the list to be offered safeguards and formal education. She reveals the campaigners who set out to tackle this injustice, including George Smith of Coalville, Leicestershire, and Sister Mary Ward of Stoke Bruerne, Northamptonshire

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Olden Days
programme.desc: The satirist explores the nation's obsession with the past to reveal how and why the British have continually plundered the 'olden days' to make sense of and shape the present. He begins by examining the various spins put on the lives of King Arthur and Alfred the Great through the ages. He finds out how Arthur has been variously depicted as a wild Celtic warlord, a chivalric hero and a piously questing king, while Alfred's character was manipulated to suit the purposes of tricky medieval lawyers, a Tudor archbishop and a Georgian Prince of Wales

programme.title: Canals: The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor examines the Manchester Ship Canal, hailed as the greatest engineering feat of the Victorian age. Widely regarded as troublesome outsiders who led a drunken life of ungodly debauchery, the navvies who built it toiled in a time of rising trade unionism, prompting Liz to consider how well they managed to organise themselves in campaigning for the best possible deal

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Autumn may be on the threshold of winter, but in this Bob Ross painting the trees - as they loom over the buttery ground - are still full of warmth and glorious colour

programme.title: Skies Above Britain
programme.desc: With several recent tragedies at air shows, two former RAF pilots - the Wildcats - must finish their display season safely. Meanwhile, a champion skydiver juggles motherhood with an intense training schedule, and a man attempts to fly Britain's first solar balloon

programme.title: Blitz: The Bombs That Changed Britain
programme.desc: The story of a tight-knit community of ship builders and factory workers in Clydebank, West Dunbartonshire, and how their lives were changed by the bombings of March 1941. The documentary focuses on the after-effects of one particular explosive, which destroyed number 78 Jellicoe Street, killing 15 members of the Rocks family. Surviving relatives and friends recall how they were deeply affected by the events, and how the return of the bombers led to a mass exodus from the town

programme.title: Cold War, Hot Jets
programme.desc: Part two of two. James Holland explores how the escalating tensions between the superpowers of America and the Soviet Union in the 1950s and 60s led to the jet bomber coming to define how the Cold War was fought. Britain's V-force aircraft were able to fly faster, higher and further than ever before, and were armed with nuclear weapons

programme.title: Great British Photography Challenge
programme.desc: Film-maker Rankin sets a series of challenges for six talented amateur photographers. In the first round, they are tested on the speed of their reactions on Brighton seafront, where they must capture stunning images using just a smart phone. They are then joined by Chris Packham in the New Forest to take shots of wildlife, and finally take a celebrity portrait of Anna Friel

programme.title: Blinded: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: As police investigate the latest death, Louise struggles to accept what has happened. Peter is made an offer, but his son, Johannes, suspects he is up to something. In Danish

programme.title: Blinded: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: Illona holds a memorial for her brother. Meanwhile, the police track down the USB stick found at Alice's and Louise comes face to face with the killer. In Danish

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Autumn may be on the threshold of winter, but in this Bob Ross painting the trees - as they loom over the buttery ground - are still full of warmth and glorious colour

programme.title: Canals: The Making of a Nation

The documentary focuses on the after-effects of one particular explosive, which destroyed number 78 Jellicoe Street, killing 15 members of the Rocks family. Surviving relatives and friends recall how they were deeply affected by the events, and how the return of the bombers led to a mass exodus from the town

programme.title: Wild
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the unusually high numbers of wild parakeets in south-east England. Estimates indicate there are more than 30,000 of the birds in the UK but experts have not been able to reach a consensus on how the creatures managed to arrive in the country

programme.title: Yorkshire Wolds Way with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Part two of two. Adventurer Paul Rose concludes his exploration of the 79-mile Yorkshire Wolds Way, taking to the skies to get a unique view of its stretch of chalk downland. He also receives a special invitation to a military base that has been keeping the nation safe since the start of the Second World War

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: The accomplished artist depicts a scene with a little cabin and shed, nestled in snowdrifts

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: A case of foot-and-mouth brings tragedy to the Dales, leaving Siegfried in quarantine and James and Tristan having to deal with all of the rounds. James discovers the power of anesthesia in assisting animals to heal - first with a farmer's dying ewe and then with the Flaxtons' pet poodle

programme.title: Masked and Anonymous (2003)
programme.desc: Crooked publicists come up with a guaranteed money-maker as they set out to spring an ageing rock star (Bob Dylan, who also co-wrote the script) from prison so he can headline a charity concert. But a canny journalist cottons on to the cynicism driving their scheme and soon looks set to thwart their plans. John Goodman and Jessica Lange co-star in this satire

programme.title: Tangled Up with Dylan: The Ballad of AJ Weberman
programme.desc: Documentary chronicling the life of notorious Bob Dylan enthusiast AJ Weberman, who tried to gain access to the singer-songwriter's psyche by analysing his rubbish. Featuring a recorded phone conversation between the duo from the 1970s and testimonies from Weberman's friends, the programme provides an insight into how far fans are willing to go to know more about their heroes

programme.title: Me, My Selfie and I with Ryan Gander
programme.desc: Cconceptual artist Ryan Gander investigates the selfie - the icon of a new kind of self-regard that hardly existed just 10 years ago. He discovers the roots of the selfie go back hundreds of years before smartphones. In the age of social media, he investigates what that really means and what technology is doing to people's sense of self

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: Dr Adam Rutherford travels to the Hague and Amsterdam to find out what inspired artists such as Rembrandt and Ruysch to create anatomical paintings in the 17th century, and learns how dissecting bodies became considered a suitable subject for art in the period

programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Alara, Samaa, Reece, Eleanor, Ty’lana and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag is are some scented lavender bags, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that are smelly. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate smelly objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The children’s nursery rhyme Ring A Ring O' Roses features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring smelly things too. The Action Song gets everyone moving about before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

After a short picnic, the family feeds the ducks and geese before Leo tries a rice cake for the very first time. At the petting farm, Erin and her family get the chance to feed some goats and hold a baby chick. The family have a picnic with juicy oranges and scrumptious sandwiches, while Erin has a bottle of milk. Leo’s family head home, where mum and Charlotte make a tasting platter for Leo, filled with fresh foods and a yummy yoghurt! After learning all about Vaisakhi, Armardas heads home to help his mum cook delicious treats for the big ceremony at the Gurdwara. Once he arrives at the Gurdwara, he begins by helping to lower and clean the flag; the Nishan Sahib. When the flag is clean, Armadas joins his dad on stage to drum and worship. programme.title: Garden Rescue

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: With her shocked guests watching on, Kheerat tries to apologise but Sharon directs her fury toward her brother - she wants Zack to pack his bags and leave. A comment from Vi has Kim worrying that she's getting old before her time, so she promptly asks out a man out for dinner, and Jean implores Ruby to show Lily some love

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The day arrives for Dom to overcome the traumas of the recent past by stepping up to consultant level. But how far is he willing to go to ensure his path to power? A desperate Kian may have to betray Fletch's trust to get what he wants, while Louis bears the brunt of Max's fear over her diagnosis

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich come up with new designs for a Hampshire garden, and must please both owners by incorporating a Greek design and an outdoor model railway

programme.title: The Pact
programme.desc: Nancy learns some disturbing news about Richard and confronts him, only to be rocked by his admission. Unsure how to remedy the situation, she's drawn closer to Fr Martin, and makes a rash decision. Cat and Tish try to get rid of Louie's stolen goods, while Louie struggles with the news about Arwel and faces a terrible dilemma. As the police make a significant breakthrough, Max tries to grill Anna on what she knows about Jack's death - putting their marriage on the line

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The MUAs enter the world of special effects, as they are tasked with recreating looks from the smash hit crime drama Peaky Blinders - working at a prestigious make-up academy with guest judge, Peaky's Bafta-winning head make-up designer Loz Scathio. This week's Creative Brief is based on the seven deadly sins, and while one artist's gruesome looks bring out sloth, envy and wrath in very imaginative ways, one MUA's literal interpretation of greed falls flat

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: When 22-year-old Bobbie Edwards had to close her gym Colwyn Bay under lockdown, she launched women-only glutes boot camp courses instead and here reveals the secrets of its success. programme.title: Garden Rescue

Saima wants a zoned garden that gives her an adult area to entertain as well as a chillout space for the youngsters and has a £2,500 budget for the conversion

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Paul Martin explore antique shops in the West Country. At auction, an expensive biscuit tin leaves a foul taste while a cheap oil painting makes an impression

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. With Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A Panorama investigation uncovering new evidence of A.I. being used by police in China to recognise the emotions of detainees, in order to help determine guilt or innocence. China has vowed to become the world's A.I. superpower by 2030, sparking a new arms race with America, and both countries are pouring billions into cutting-edge military tech, including autonomous weapons. A.I. could usher in a golden age, but without urgent regulation, experts warn we could lose control of artificial intelligence, a prospect, they say, that should scare us all

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steven Fletcher tackles an intricate Boulle-work clock that has not ticked for more than 15 years, and soft toy restorers Julie Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch take on a threadbare wheeled elephant. Woodwork specialist William Kirk gets to grips with one of the show's oldest assignments in the form of a 300-year-old Georgian desk that is showing its age

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett hosts as temperatures drop and winter hits the sewing room, with challenges themed around keeping warm and adding sparkle to the coldest months of the year. Patrick and Esme's Pattern Challenge this week is a man's flannel shirt. The trickiest pattern challenge yet, the sewers must create cuffs and collars, challenging both their construction technique and precise sewing skills

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Bethan Rhys Roberts presents the weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters

programme.title: Scam City: Money, Mayhem and Maseratis
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar investigates the story of a 20-year-old medical student, who went from handing out cash to strangers to being at the centre of an alleged multi-million-pound scam

programme.title: Scam City: Money, Mayhem and Maseratis
programme.desc: Searching for the elusive Gurvin, Mobeen Azhar hopes to find answers for the people who believe they were scammed - revealing a common thread that blows the story wide open

programme.title: Subnormal: A British Scandal
programme.desc: In the 1960s, West Indian migrants to the UK counted on providing better opportunities for their children. However, there are suggestions that before having a chance to develop intellectually, their offspring were labelled as 'stupid, difficult and disruptive' as psychologists such as Hans Eysenck and Arthur Jensen theorised that black people were genetically less intelligent than white. IQ tests were then based on these theories with the odds stacked against them. Featuring first-hand interviews with key participants of the time, this documentary examines what is potentially one of the biggest scandals in the history of British education

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits Staffordshire to investigate a damp and dingy two-bed terrace, before heading to Rochdale, Greater Manchester, where he discovers a neglected detached house with some beautiful period features. programme.title: Fraud Squad

programme.title: Unbeatable

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to the Scottish Borders with a couple who want their £450,000 budget to buy them an equestrian property in the country. Jules also visits the dramatic ruins of Melrose Abbey, where the heart of Scotland's medieval king Robert the Bruce is believed to be buried

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and Harry and David Rich help Bath resident Mina, who wants to turn her characterless, overgrown lawn into a Persian paradise. They have a budget of £2,700 to create something Mina will be proud of, and which is evocative of her childhood in the Middle East, including colourful and aromatic planting, stunning tiles, fruit trees and an Iranian-inspired water feature

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Paul Martin visit places including Dorset, Devon and Somerset. Along the way, Paul investigates the obscure, Somerset-based Victorian inventor of unmanned powered flight, while Raj finds out about The Bristol Bus Boycott, a 1960s struggle against racial discrimination. Both experts make profitable finds, with a mule-based fairground ride and an exquisite English oak candle box the star lots at auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mila worries about protecting Kioni as Kathy orders Bobby to look after her, while Vinny wonders about his brother's mystery woman but Kheerat refuses to kiss and tell. He later runs into Sharon, who mentions she's free all day but Kheerat isn't keen - until he sees Gray and suddenly changes his mind. Keegan leaves Sonia with some food for thought

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A London-based entrepreneur with an assortment of naturally derived sugar alternatives hopes he can pull off a sweet deal, while a couple face a roasting over their optimistic projections for their vitamin-infused coffee. Multimillionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies are also approached by an inventor who hopes to fix a deal with her portable children's suction mats and consider investing in a business selling innovative eco-cleaning products. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: What Are We Feeding Our Kids?
programme.desc: Across the world childhood obesity rates have risen tenfold in 50 years. In the UK 21 per cent of children are living with obesity when they leave primary school - the highest it's ever been. Ultra-processed food is cheap and convenient, and accounts for the majority of young people's calorie intake - 64 per cent for children and 67 per cent for teenagers. Yet alarmingly, scientists know very little about exactly how it affects youngsters' bodies. Dr Chris van Tulleken investigates the impact of ultra-processed food, as he undergoes a gruelling self-experiment that even shocks the scientists

programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public
