programme.desc: Eric Knowles hosts from Lincolnshire Antiques and Home Show as experts Jonathan Pratt and Gary Pe assist the teams racing around the fair pursuing potential purchases for profit at auction. Eric also shares some top tips on collecting Clarice Cliff pottery

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Luca is on the hunt for Jonno, but he may not like what he finds. Al tries to mend the difficult relationship between a wildly different mother and son

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Dion, James, Marilyn and Penny vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah visits Scotland, making her way to Dumfries & Galloway with a married couple from London who are seeking a life-changing move and have a £350,000 budget

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich meet Liz, Geoff and their daughter Lois in Yate, Gloucestershire, where the hope is they can use a £4,500 budget to create a garden with a nautical theme in memory of Liz's father, who was known locally as ''The Pirate'

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The trip continues in Wiltshire for debutant Paul Martin and seasoned chum Raj Bisram, but a risky buy of antique jelly moulds causes an auction wobble for one expert

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. With Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: With her shocked guests watching on, Kheerat tries to apologise but Sharon directs her fury toward her brother - she wants Zack to pack his bags and leave. A comment from Vi has Kim worrying that she's getting old before her time, so she promptly asks out a man out for dinner, and Jean implores Ruby to show Lily some love

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The day arrives for Dom to overcome the traumas of the recent past by stepping up to consultant level. But how far is he willing to go to ensure his path to power? A desperate Kian may have to betray Fletch's trust to get what he wants, while Louis bears the brunt of Max's fear over her diagnosis

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate the case of a man in Scotland whose online girlfriend in Ghana has demanded thousands of pounds, but has never visited him

programme.title: The Pact
programme.desc: Nancy learns some disturbing news about Richard and confronts him, only to be rocked by his admission. Unsure how to remedy the situation, she's drawn closer to Fr Martin, and makes a rash decision. Cat and Tish try to get rid of Louie's stolen goods, while Louie struggles with the news about Arwel and faces a terrible dilemma. As the police make a significant breakthrough, Max tries to grill Anna on what she knows about Jack's death - putting their marriage on the line

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: How a £1 deal for public land on the scenic Causeway Coast put an ardent unionist and a staunch republican on the same side of a planning fight. Conor Spackman investigates

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The MUAs enter the world of special effects, as they are tasked with recreating looks from the smash hit crime drama Peaky Blinders - working at a prestigious make-up academy with guest judge, Peaky's Bafta-winning head make-up designer Loz Scathio. This week's Creative Brief is based on the seven deadly sins, and while one artist's gruesome looks bring out sloth, envy and wrath in very imaginative ways, one MUA's literal interpretation of greed falls flat

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma has a big mess to take care of on her arrival home, Norman agrees to seek help, and Dylan receives shocking news about Emma's health

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Caleb and Dylan are confronted by a dangerous proposition, and Sheriff Romero decides to confront Norma

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts views a ground-floor flat in London, Martel Maxwell checks out a former commercial unit in Staffordshire and Dion Dublin look at a three-bedroom semi in Birmingham

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: Fraud specialists seek a serial scam artist faking disability to claim thousands of pounds in insurance payouts, and also uncover the most prolific dental fraudster ever. Plus, a preacher turns prisoner when he's caught with his hand in the public purse. Presented by Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Stories of life in the balance, with Nick Knowles. A father-to-be has a horrific accident while mountainbiking with friends. A man braves a fire at home to search for his dogs

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts, as experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell help two teams find items of value in Nottingham. Anita also goes on a personal journey to see the works of Dame Laura Knight, one of her favourite artists

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: A detoxing Jonno turns on Luca, while Ruhma tries to get Sid to unwrap his feelings. A locked car and a dead phone battery push Zara to the limit

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Hannah, John, Helen and Vince vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple looking for a property in Gloucestershire on a budget of £850,000 and visits an ice cream-making family on Rodburgh Common

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Swindon to meet Saima and her children Hamzah, Hamid and Zakir, who are rather embarrassed by their concrete jungle of a garden. Saima wants a zoned garden that gives her an adult area to entertain as well as a chillout space for the youngsters and has a £2,500 budget for the conversion

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Paul Martin explore antique shops in the West Country. At auction, an expensive biscuit tin leaves a foul taste while a cheap oil painting makes an impression

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. With Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A Panorama investigation uncovering new evidence of A.I. being used by police in China to recognise the emotions of detainees, in order to help determine guilt or innocence. China has vowed to become the world's A.I. superpower by 2030, sparking a new arms race with America, and both countries are pouring billions into cutting-edge military tech, including autonomous weapons. A.I. could usher in a golden age, but without urgent regulation, experts warn we could lose control of artificial intelligence, a prospect, they say, that should scare us all

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steven Fletcher tackles an intricate Boulle-work clock that has not ticked for more than 15 years, and soft toy restorers Julie Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch take on a threadbare wheeled elephant. Woodwork specialist William Kirk gets to grips with one of the show's oldest assignments in the form of a 300-year-old Georgian desk that is showing its age

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett hosts as temperatures drop and winter hits the sewing room, with challenges themed around keeping warm and adding sparkle to the coldest months of the year. Patrick and Esme's Pattern Challenge this week is a man's flannel shirt. The trickiest pattern challenge yet, the sewers must create cuffs and collars, challenging both their construction technique and precise sewing skills

programme.title: Nolan Live
programme.desc: Stephen Nolan probes the issues that matter to the people of Northern Ireland, focusing on the stories of the week and the people making the headlines in lively debates, with viewers sending in their questions via text, email and tweet

programme.title: Scam City: Money, Mayhem and Maseratis
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar investigates the story of a 20-year-old medical student, who went from handing out cash to strangers to being at the centre of an alleged multi-million-pound scam

programme.title: Scam City: Money, Mayhem and Maseratis
programme.desc: Searching for the elusive Gurvin, Mobeen Azhar hopes to find answers for the people who believe they were scammed - revealing a common thread that blows the story wide open

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: When 22-year-old Bobbie Edwards had to close her gym Colwyn Bay under lockdown, she launched women-only glutes boot camp courses instead and here reveals the secrets of its success. Bridgend-based entrepreneur Scott Magill reveals how he struck lockdown-gold with his doorstep doughnut delivery service

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits Staffordshire to investigate a damp and dingy two-bed terrace, before heading to Rochdale, Greater Manchester, where he discovers a neglected detached house with some beautiful period features. Meanwhile, Martel Maxwell goes to Hertfordshire to find out just what can be done with a pair of old garages

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: A locksmith sees a chance to make quick gains when he is put in charge of a London hospital - and pockets nearly half a million pounds by overcharging the hospital for supplies. Elsewhere, an NHS manager dreams up a scam to siphon over £800,000 of NHS money into his own account. Presented by Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: The emergency services attend a baby who has cut his throat on a glass. Plus, a woman is knocked to the ground and trampled after a dog spooks horses. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant guide two teams scouring the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells for bargains to take to auction. Presenter Charlie Ross monitors their progress and also finds out about a weird and wonderful collection of bicycles

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie's team appreciation gift exchange leads to some big decisions, while Ruhma supports Sid on the day of Lewis' wake. Jimmi helps a struggling student to mend his relationship with his strictly vegan ex-girlfriend

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Andy, Stuart, Alice and Charlotte vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to the Scottish Borders with a couple who want their £450,000 budget to buy them an equestrian property in the country. Jules also visits the dramatic ruins of Melrose Abbey, where the heart of Scotland's medieval king Robert the Bruce is believed to be buried

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and Harry and David Rich help Bath resident Mina, who wants to turn her characterless, overgrown lawn into a Persian paradise. They have a budget of £2,700 to create something Mina will be proud of, and which is evocative of her childhood in the Middle East, including colourful and aromatic planting, stunning tiles, fruit trees and an Iranian-inspired water feature

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Paul Martin visit places including Dorset, Devon and Somerset. Along the way, Paul investigates the obscure, Somerset-based Victorian inventor of unmanned powered flight, while Raj finds out about The Bristol Bus Boycott, a 1960s struggle against racial discrimination. Both experts make profitable finds, with a mule-based fairground ride and an exquisite English oak candle box the star lots at auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mila worries about protecting Kioni as Kathy orders Bobby to look after her, while Vinny wonders about his brother's mystery woman but Kheerat refuses to kiss and tell. He later runs into Sharon, who mentions she's free all day but Kheerat isn't keen - until he sees Gray and suddenly changes his mind. Keegan leaves Sonia with some food for thought

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A London-based entrepreneur with an assortment of naturally derived sugar alternatives hopes he can pull off a sweet deal, while a couple face a roasting over their optimistic projections for their vitamin-infused coffee. Multimillionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies are also approached by an inventor who hopes to fix a deal with her portable children's suction mats and consider investing in a business selling innovative eco-cleaning products. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: The Road to Partition
programme.desc: Part one of two. On June 22, 1921, King George V and Queen Mary arrived in Belfast for the official opening of the first Northern Ireland parliament. Fearful for their lives, they had come to a city scarred by bitter sectarian violence. One hundred years on, this is the story of the dramatic events that led to the partition of Ireland - one that continues to reverberate to the present day

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: A review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests. Presented by Mark Carruthers

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic heptathlon champion Denise Lewis, rugby league great Paul Sculthorpe, former England striker Kevin Phillips and British javelin star Goldie Sayers join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Monday's business in the House of Lords including questions to government ministers, statements on the Post Office and on the railway network as well as the second reading of the Leasehold Reform (Ground Rent) Bill

programme.title: Monday in Parliament
programme.desc: Highlights of the day's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Public Administration and Constitutional Affairs select committee session on the work of the Electoral Commission with evidence from the former key officials from campaigns on both sides of the Brexit referendum as well as former MPs Dominic Grieve and Baroness Stuart of Edgbaston.

programme.desc: Live coverage of questions in the House of Commons to the Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy Secretary Kwasi Kwarteng and his team of ministers

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Gudrun - A' Bhana-phrionnsa Lochlannach
programme.desc: When a raven gets trapped in a basket, Gudrun comes to the rescue. Later, she needs its help when she loses her precious circlet made of Viking silver

programme.title: Peataichean/Pets
programme.desc: Eilidh Cormack is joined by Sally and Helen and their rabbits, Jasper and Delilah

programme.title: Sionnach agus Maigheach (Fox & Hare)
programme.desc: Owl wants to enter a drawing contest, but fearing his work is not good enough, he throws it away. Fox brings the discarded drawing to Hare and they decide to send it in secretly

programme.title: Shane an Chef
programme.desc: Mama Polenta requires a very special recipe, as she hunts the magic ingredients for a conditioner that will give her fabulous hair ahead of the big awards ceremony

programme.title: Treud na Dlùth-choille - Grad-Naidheachd (Jungle Bunch)
programme.desc: Although Gibidh is quick to complain about others, a pleasant surprise awaits

programme.title: Abadas
programme.desc: Ela, Hari and Seren play the word game after racing their karts around the garden

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Eoghan Stewart reads Pom Pom Stùirceach, written and illustrated by Sophy Henn and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart

programme.title: Pìobairean Lionacleit ann an NY (Lionacleit Pipers in NY)
programme.desc: The Sgoil Lionacleit pipe band travel on a trip of a lifetime to the United States of America. The highlight of their trip will be to perform alongside scores of bands from around the world at the annual Tartan Day Parade, which takes place on 6th Avenue, New York City, in front of thousands of onlookers. The players also pay tribute to their friend and band member Eilidh MacLeod, who died in the Manchester Arena bombing of May 2017

programme.title: An t-Eilean (From Skye with Love)
programme.desc: Life in the Trotternish peninsula on the island of Skye through the seasons. The Isle of Skye Highland Games are held in Portree, Donald and Ally gather the sheep for shearing in Duntulm and the Skye first shinty team travel to Cannich for an important game

programme.title: Ever Believe Me - Sgeulachd Madeleine Smith (The Madeleine Smith Story)
programme.desc: Documentary revisiting the 19th-century murder case involving Madeleine Smith, asking whether the Glasgow socialite really did poison her lover with arsenic. Presented by John Morrison

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: Uisdean and Roddy head out of the kitchen, one to the country and one to the sea, returning with venison sausages for a casserole and a wealth of seafood for a platter

programme.title: Billy Graham
programme.desc: This film looks at the life and politics of the charismatic Billy Graham, America's 'superstar' pastor, who gathered thousands of faithfuls during his 'televangelisation' campaigns

programme.title: Binneas

programme.title: Good Morning Britain

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: More celebrity interviews and topical debate from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson presents the show from the Devonshire Dome in Buxton, where finds brought in for auction include the death mask of romantic poet John Keats, an unusual corkscrew, and an art deco diamond watch

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of five called Young's Offenders answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge. Then they try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Andy, Heather, Ahmed and Nichola pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Things escalate at the Dingles', Liv watches a heartfelt message, and Ben wants to help Aaron

programme.title: Ainsley's Mediterranean Cookbook
programme.desc: Ainsley continues to explore the glorious Mediterranean region of southern Spain, making his way to the gastronomic hotspot that is Granada. Here, he learns all about the famous Alhambra Palace and samples a sweet local delicacy, piononos, with tour guide Katia. Later, he has a lesson in Flamenco dancing, before rustling up a classic seafood paella

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the big-prize quiz in which another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with the lifelines Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50-50 and Ask the Host available to them if they get stuck

programme.title: Long Lost Family: Born Without Trace
programme.desc: Time is running out for one foundling who was discovered on a luggage rack in Paddington station nearly 75 years ago, while another woman hopes DNA will solve the mystery of why she was left next to a church in Liverpool at two days old. Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: On Assignment
programme.desc: Lucrezia Millarini travels to the Spanish island of Ibiza to find out how they might be able to welcome back British tourists this summer. Steve Scott travels to Qatar to see why the falcon continues to be the most highly prized and looked-after bird in the Gulf. Plus, Rageh Omaar is in Montenegro, home to some of Europe's last free-flowing bodies of water, to find out if small hydropower plants that promise cleaner energy also be polluting the pristine waterways of this Balkan state

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. In 2019, the airport is having its busiest year to date, and are expecting more than 81 million passengers to fly in and out of the terminals, with its 76,000 staff ready to take on whatever is thrown at them

programme.title: Cheat
programme.desc: With relations becoming increasingly hostile, Leah resorts to more extreme measures to expose Rose, and as tensions flare between Leah and Adam, could it be that her husband is taking Rose's side? Later, Rose seems to pursue a more personal retaliation against Leah, but who is telling the truth? Katherine Kelly, Molly Windsor and Tom Goodman-Hill star

programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Omid Djalili host the quiz in which nine contestants - Byron, Melanie, Harry, Sarah, Louise, Eddie, Simon, Naoise and James - are each assigned a number between one and nine, with the four that make the final trying to win the jackpot defined by those numbers. Each player's number is also the total of questions they must answer correctly in the final game against the clock

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Natasha demands that Nick calls the police and tells them the truth about Sam's kidnap. Cathy admits to Brian that her hoarding is out of control again. Jenny confronts Daisy about how she tried to blackmail Ronnie

programme.title: Martin Lewis' Extreme Savers
programme.desc: Martin Lewis meets people who go to the extremes to save money. In the third episode, Martin meets a couple who are living off the grid in a converted horse box, and a man who avoids the high costs of airline baggage by wearing a coat of many pockets. Later, he also meets a bargain hunter who shares tips on how to bag designer clothes at a fraction of the price

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Shona informs Natasha that by involving the police, she will be putting Sam in even more danger. Tim tells Sally that he has invited Elaine to live with them. Steve and Emma put Curtis through a mock interview

programme.title: Long Lost Family: Born Without Trace
programme.desc: A woman born in a hospital but whose birth mother disappeared, never to be traced, discovers a shocking secret, while a man, left as a baby near Wolverhampton Wanderers' Stadium and named after a famous footballer, searches for his true identity. Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell

programme.title: Peston
programme.desc: Political magazine show presented by Robert Peston, featuring major interviews with MPs, topical guests and cultural figures. Including updates from social media taking in perspectives from viewers, experts and key players throughout the programme

programme.title: Cheat
programme.desc: Leah deals with the fallout of Adam's betrayal, but some unexpected news further complicates matters. A distraction comes in the form of Michael and Angela's anniversary party, but as Rose's behaviour becomes progressively more volatile, the truth is finally exposed over the course of one explosive evening, with devastating consequences. Starring Katherine Kelly and Molly Windsor

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim is terrified, Tracy is mortified, and Manpreet is gutted

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: For many of us, months of lockdown has meant taking a long hard look at our surroundings and tackling the clutter in our homes. But what do we do with all of our unwanted stuff? Reporter Angellica Bell investigates

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Leyla and Andrea are worried. Wendy checks in on Tracy. Nate lends an ear

programme.title: Australia with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia reaches South Australia as she continues her 12,000km journey across the continent. She drives along the Explorers Way, a route through the Outback mapped out by a pioneer in 1862, to the world's most isolated pub, where a small plane takes her on to the ancient landscape of the Painted Hills. In Adelaide, she climbs the roof of the Oval sports stadium and meets a women's cycling team, before ending her journey in wine country

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Anne Clegg and her mother Janet were first reunited nearly a decade ago. She reveals the impact their successful mother-daughter relationship has had on her life. The team also catches up with grandmother Alice Jones, who was originally looking for her younger brother Sam. And there is an update on Sharon Harte and her sisters, Carole, Terri and Sandra

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Unbelievable Moments Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong presents a selection of extraordinary eyewitness footage filmed by members of the public, including the heart-stopping story of a man who took a 'pleasure flight' on a hang-glider. Two passengers on board the Viking Sky cruise ship that suffered engine failure in rough seas just off the coast of Norway recount how they were left fearing for their lives, while a heroic cop throws the rulebook out of the window when trying to save a baby's life on the roadside

programme.title: Cheat
programme.desc: As the police try to piece together the fatal sequence of events, both women come under the spotlight, but have they got the right one? The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.desc: Julia reaches South Australia as she continues her 12,000km journey across the continent. She drives along the Explorers Way, a route through the Outback mapped out by a pioneer in 1862, to the world's most isolated pub, where a small plane takes her on to the ancient landscape of the Painted Hills. In Adelaide, she climbs the roof of the Oval sports stadium and meets a women's cycling team, before ending her journey in wine country

Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell, with Charles Dance and Maggie Steed

programme.desc: Des tackles Samantha about her lies about Natalie. Judy tells Jim that Gary has walked out on her. Greg offers to listen to Sally's marriage problems

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. The manner of her death creates a sensation in the tense village community of Little Ambrose, but although the woman's husband confesses to the crime, Miss Marple uncovers new evidence that could affect the court hearing's outcome and races to halt proceedings before the wrong person is convicted. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When the sleuth's dentist is found dead just minutes after he leaves his surgery, Chief Inspector Japp believes it to be suicide - but Poirot has reason to think otherwise and embarks on his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Tommy Hicks of the Midsomer Yeomanry finally has his name added to the Midsomer Parva war memorial, though he was shot for cowardice at the Somme in the First World War. It becomes apparent that time has not healed the rift between his descendants and the residents of Hammond Hall, whose patriarch Duggie Hammond pulled the trigger on his comrade all those years ago. A drink-fuelled mock trial of retired colonel Henry Hammond leads to a killing, and Tom Barnaby has to unravel the tangle of bitterness linking property developer Dave Hicks's brood with their neighbours. Drama, guest starring Donald Sinden and George Cole, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The unassuming detective launches an investigation when a conscientious objector dies while in police custody, and the judge who ordered his arrest is nearly killed in his booby-trapped home. His inquiries uncover a web of lies and corruption in the heart of wartime Hastings. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and David Tennant

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour detective Jack Frost investigates a woman's near-fatal shooting. The victim was all set to testify against a notorious drugs baron accused of murdering one of his employees, but the appearance of a contract killer makes her think again - and it appears someone on Jack's team is leaking information. Meanwhile, the station is turned upside down in the hunt for a missing lottery ticket. David Jason, Michael McKell and Bruce Alexander star

programme.desc: Morse is assigned to protect an informant who is undergoing emergency surgery. Second World War crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Julian Ovenden, Honeysuckle Weeks, Anthony Calf, Roger Allam and Anton Lesser

Crime drama, starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Caroline Harker and Maurice Roeves

programme.desc: Emily helps out on the Dingles' farm. Bernice is looking forward to taking over the Woolpack. Donna pleads with Scott to put things right with Kelly

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick is furious when Leanne tells him she has visited Darren in prison. Emily signs up to be Spider's campaign manager. Judy is thrilled when Gary turns up

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Roy receives a letter from Hayley saying she is living in Amsterdam. Leanne gives Nick an ultimatum about going to the police. Spider's electoral campaign hits an obstacle

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When a spate of petty thefts culminates in a series of murders at a student hostel, Miss Lemon persuades the Belgian sleuth to investigate. As ever, he receives support from Chief Inspector Japp. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Philip Jackson, Pauline Moran, Damian Lewis and Paris Jefferson

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective reopens an unsolved case when a skeleton is discovered beneath the burned-out remains of a Whitley Bay nightclub. She suspects the bones belong to a notorious criminal who was once a friend of her father and has been missing for more than 20 years. When another murder occurs nearby, she investigates the possibility that a local crime family may be involved with both deaths, while the original owners of the club also have secrets to hide. Brenda Blethyn stars in an adaptation of Ann Cleeves' 2017 Vera Stanhope novel The Seagull

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The year is 1940, and Sam is made homeless when a bomb destroys her boarding house, alerting Foyle to a system of organised looting around Hastings. However, when a corpse is found on the beach, the investigation brings the team into conflict with British attempts to secure vital American aid for the war effort. Police drama, starring Michael Kitchen, with Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack learns he is tipped for promotion with the uniform branch and even more flattered to discover he has a secret admirer, but before he has time to bask in this rare moment of glory a child murder claims his attention. Linking the disappearance of a 10-year-old boy to a mysterious hit-and-run killing, he is soon mired in a complex investigation and fuming about a bureaucratic blunder that leaves a known paedophile walking free on his patch. David Jason stars

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: An old flame of Mildred's turns up at the Roper house, prompting George to blow the cobwebs off his little black book. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As the Second World War continues to rage, Foyle investigates a pro-Hitler organisation he suspects may be linked to a mysterious death at the White Feather Hotel, while reluctant assistant Milner struggles to decide where his loyalties lie. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell, with Charles Dance and Maggie Steed

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A kidnapping case takes an unexpected turn, placing the entire village in danger. Meanwhile, Nick meets new schoolteacher Jo, and Blaketon enjoys his status as president of the local rugby club - until Greengrass takes an interest

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernice cannot believe Stella's kindness. Roy tries to calm Kelly down about Scott. Emily sneaks out of the house when her dad is asleep

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The villagers prepare for the cricket match. Ashley chats to Bernice about Stella's warning. Butch takes Emily home but they are ambushed by John with a shotgun

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Judy tries to locate Gary, but no one knows where he is. Kevin demands Sally tells him what he has done wrong. Fred tries to get close to Audrey by volunteering for her campaign

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des tackles Samantha about her lies about Natalie. Judy tells Jim that Gary has walked out on her. Greg offers to listen to Sally's marriage problems

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. The manner of her death creates a sensation in the tense village community of Little Ambrose, but although the woman's husband confesses to the crime, Miss Marple uncovers new evidence that could affect the court hearing's outcome and races to halt proceedings before the wrong person is convicted. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When the sleuth's dentist is found dead just minutes after he leaves his surgery, Chief Inspector Japp believes it to be suicide - but Poirot has reason to think otherwise and embarks on his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Tommy Hicks of the Midsomer Yeomanry finally has his name added to the Midsomer Parva war memorial, though he was shot for cowardice at the Somme in the First World War. It becomes apparent that time has not healed the rift between his descendants and the residents of Hammond Hall, whose patriarch Duggie Hammond pulled the trigger on his comrade all those years ago. A drink-fuelled mock trial of retired colonel Henry Hammond leads to a killing, and Tom Barnaby has to unravel the tangle of bitterness linking property developer Dave Hicks's brood with their neighbours. Drama, guest starring Donald Sinden and George Cole, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The unassuming detective launches an investigation when a conscientious objector dies while in police custody, and the judge who ordered his arrest is nearly killed in his booby-trapped home. His inquiries uncover a web of lies and corruption in the heart of wartime Hastings. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and David Tennant

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour detective Jack Frost investigates a woman's near-fatal shooting. The victim was all set to testify against a notorious drugs baron accused of murdering one of his employees, but the appearance of a contract killer makes her think again - and it appears someone on Jack's team is leaking information. Meanwhile, the station is turned upside down in the hunt for a missing lottery ticket. David Jason, Michael McKell and Bruce Alexander star

programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster is invited to the wedding of her former housemaid Gwenda, only to be caught up in a murder mystery involving the groom's family. Geraldine McEwan stars, with Juliet Stevenson, Denis Lawson and Alison Steadman

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper detective accepts an invitation to spend yuletide at the house of an elderly millionaire who seems intent on provoking his money-grubbing sons into murdering him. At first he tries hard to ignore the strained atmosphere and relax - but soon finds himself with a gruesome mystery on his hands. Agatha Christie whodunit, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse is assigned to protect an informant who is undergoing emergency surgery. The case brings back unpleasant memories for the detective, while his colleagues are thrown into disarray when Bright is also rushed to hospital. Morse becomes fixated with a series of mysterious deaths that have occurred on the ward, and tries to find the scientific explanation behind stories of a curse

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective is faced with a dilemma when a murder investigation leads him to the radar station where his missing son was working on a highly classified operation - just as Hitler launches a major offensive on Britain's RAF airfields. To solve the mystery, Foyle must penetrate the closed ranks of the airforce. Second World War crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Julian Ovenden, Honeysuckle Weeks, Anthony Calf, Roger Allam and Anton Lesser

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The investigation of a burglary at a quarry turns into something far more serious when a security guard's badly beaten body is discovered at the scene of the crime. An autistic teenager is arrested for the murder, despite Inspector Frost's misgivings - a move which triggers a chain of tragic events culminating in a tense stand-off. Crime drama, starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Caroline Harker and Maurice Roeves

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the case of a man who has acquired a well-paid but mysterious job - seemingly on account of his red hair. Period detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett and David Burke, with Eric Porter as Moriarty

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed and Emma visit the countryside to investigate rumours of a ghost seen in the private chapel of a long-dead duke supposedly killed in a mining disaster. Julian Glover guest stars

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: When a CIA agent dies after being thrown off a balcony, the unit sets up a fake arms deal with the German terrorist cell responsible. However, the gunrunners uncover the ploy, kidnap Bodie and transform him into a walking time-bomb. Lewis Collins stars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur's prestige as a pillar of the community is boosted by an invitation to address a meeting of his local Rotary Club - which is being held in the very hotel where Terry is acting as temporary house detective. However, a pair of thieves posing as priests and a mangeress equally as crooked as Arthur threaten to ruin his grand moment. Drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Extreme Salvage Squad
programme.desc: The team heads to the seaside town of Rockingham in Western Australia to try and salvage a 34-foot yacht called the Renee Louise. It's run aground and the owner has gone AWOL. With a major tourist event kicking off in three days, the local council is desperate to get the vessel off the beach - it's an eyesore and it's dangerous. The team has two days to get the job done.

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Embarrassing Bodies presenter Christian Jessen, JB Gill of JLS, comedian Helen Lederer and former professional snooker player Stephen Hendry pit their wits against the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for their charity of choice. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: World Superbike Highlights
programme.desc: Highlights of the opening round of the campaign, held at Ciudad del Motor de Aragon in Spain

programme.desc: James Bond clashes with a megalomaniac international businessman plotting a global takeover of the computer industry by engineering a devastating earthquake in California's Silicon Valley - and killing millions in the process. Spy adventure, starring Roger Moore in his final appearance as 007 alongside Christopher Walken, Grace Jones, Tanya Roberts and Patrick Macnee

programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Pastry chefs from hotels, restaurants and small businesses in Cardiff, Bristol, Norfolk, Brighton and London compete to be crowned best patisserie team in Britain. The teams face testing challenges judged by two world-class patisserie experts, Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden. For their first challenge, the teams must produce two different types of miniature classics - Tarte Piémontaise and the Jaffa Cake. They must make 24 perfect and identical items of each pastry in just three and a half hours

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows more of Britain's most ambitious self-building projects, as intrepid individuals attempt to design and construct the home of their dreams. In this first edition, Kevin meets former army captain Justin, who has spent £1.8million on an abandoned neo-Gothic cemetery keeper's lodge and adjoining ex-council toilet block in south-west London. Justin's vision is to restore the lodge but demolish the ugly toilet blocks and replace them with an enormous luxury ground-floor extension, with an added moat and a giant basement with a swimming pool - all just metres from the nearest grave

programme.title: The Money Maker
programme.desc: Eric Collins meets Darren Tenkorang, the founder of Trim-It, a mobile barber service that can be booked via an app. Eric knows the company has huge potential but can't understand why they are growing so slowly? With The Money Maker's help, the business could be ten times more successful, but only if they take his advice and act upon it quickly before potential competitors steal Trim-It's march. With no time to lose, Eric puts Darren under pressure to accept his investment, but will they agree on the best way forward?

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: A look back at the times when what started as great chemistry in the studio ended in dating disaster, reliving memorable arguments and cringeworthy moments, including bisexual Leah from the third series, who brought her pet dog Kush to the studio to help her pick her partner. There is also a reunion between Cathy from Hertfordshire and her date Andy

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef tries to reverse the fortunes of the Mixing Bowl diner in Bellmore, New York, where a talented cook's work is being undone by the manager's marketing plans - putting the finances in dire straits

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef heads to the Seascape restaurant in the village of Islip on Long Island, New York, in a bid to change the fortunes of its owner, who is nearly $1million in debt. Gordon is dismayed to discover unprotected meat and fish left next to mouldy pesto jars in the kitchen and plans to close down the eatery until radical changes are made

programme.title: SAS: Who Dares Wins
programme.desc: The recruits are tested on how well they can control their emotions by three tests, including a backward dive at night, an illusion jump and a challenge designed to push their ability to keep their aggression in check. programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The proud residents of Springfield are outraged when they learn their town anthem was stolen from a travelling musician, so Bart and Lisa volunteer to find a replacement song. Meanwhile, Homer is on a mission to find a fat people's rights group after a humiliating incident in which he gets stuck in his seat. With the guest voice of Pharrell Williams

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Cher tries to convince Mercedes to take a pregnancy test. Diane finds herself feeling overwhelmed with her due date fast approaching

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer catches up with three house-hunters with two very different needs, but one shared goal. In 2018, schoolteacher Katy, her comedian husband Charlie, baby daughter Lola, and pet pooch Harper were on the hunt for their first family home in Brighton. Having moved in with Katy's mum to save for a deposit, they had not only grown used to home comforts, but also the vibrant village atmosphere of pricey Fiveways - an area where their property dreams didn't match their budget. With Phil's help they widened the net, viewing four fantastic finds in and around Brighton. Two years later, Phil's back to see whether life on the coast has been plain sailing. And in 2019, Kirstie was scouring east London with 'granny on the go' Janet. Having been diagnosed with a degenerative back condition, Janet was desperate to ditch her lift-less, third storey flat in exchange for a ground-floor forever home. The tricky east London property market was proving tough to navigate though, and Kirstie soon f

programme.title: Before We Die
programme.desc: When her married lover and fellow police officer Sean Hardacre goes missing in mysterious circumstances, DI Hannah Laing persuades her colleagues to launch an urgent manhunt. She manages to contact one of Sean's confidential sources, but can 'Izzy' help her find him before it's too late? Crime thriller based on the hit Swedish drama of the same name, starring Lesley Sharp, Vincent Regan and Patrick Gibson

programme.title: Johnny Vegas: Carry on Glamping
programme.desc: After two gruelling years during which Johnny Vegas has seen his plans for his glampsite hit by every problem imaginable, he is finally ready for the grand unveiling. Unfortunately, it's in front of his harshest critics - his family and friends. With the final vehicles arriving, there's time for Johnny to deceive his assistant Bev one more time, before he presents her with the 1950s camper van he bought and restored as a gift. As last-minute preparations on the site continue, Johnny inspects the renovated fire truck which quickly takes a wrong turn as he manages to get stuck on the roof, and things get worse from there. As he and Bev prepare for the arrival of the much-loved Maltese bus that started the whole adventure, disaster strikes one final time

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Natalka Greenwood and Sam Watson treat a 10-year-old autistic boy who has injured his back on a trampoline. Emma Snelson and Lizzie Hobday rush to a baby who has stopped breathing

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Another trio of Halloween stories. Bart unlocks a portal to hell and attends a school where bad behaviour is actively encouraged - although goody-two-shoes Lisa has a tougher time fitting in. Homer stars in his own version of A Clockwork Orange, in which he tries to give up the thug lifestyle after falling for a certain blue-haired lady, and in the final story, the family are haunted by ghosts from the past

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Double-act Cheech and Chong split during their Springfield reunion show, leaving Homer to step into the breach and help out one of his comedy heroes. The new line-up is so successful that Cheech decides to partner Homer full-time, leaving Chong to recruit Principal Skinner as the other half of his new act

programme.title: Little Men (2016)
programme.desc: A 13-year-old boy recovering from the death of his grandfather befriends the son of a Chilean woman, who rents a dress shop from his parents. When the rent on the shop is increased, it creates a bitter feud between the two families, and the boys respond by taking a vow of silence to pressure their parents into backing down. Drama, starring Greg Kinnear and Jennifer Ehle

programme.title: We Are Lady Parts
programme.desc: Comedy charting the highs and lows of Muslim punk band Lady Parts, as seen through the eyes of Amina Hussain - a geeky microbiology PhD student, who is recruited to be their unlikely lead guitarist at the instigation of fiery frontwoman Saira, who sees something in Amina that the others cannot. Amina is torn between two different worlds and on the lookout for love, romance - and a husband. Anjana Vasan, Sarah Kameela Impey, Juliette Motamed and Lucie Shorthouse star

programme.title: The Handmaid's Tale
programme.desc: June must rely on an unpredictable Commander Lawrence to execute her plan, only for a terrifying journey to a place from her past to put everything in jeopardy. Serena Joy and Commander Waterford take a clandestine trip. Dystopian drama, starring Elisabeth Moss, Yvonne Strahovski, Joseph Fiennes and Bradley Whitford

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: A materialistic city woman who believes appearance is everything swaps lives with a charity-minded farmer's wife

programme.title: The Great Garden Revolution
programme.desc: People around the country transform outdoor spaces with the help of a team of experts

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp enters the paper crafts competition at the Royal Welsh Show. She produces an embroidered greetings card, and decoupage designer Jaina Minton teaches her how to make a paper sculpture of a seagull. To create jewellery, Phiona Richards lends a helping hand recycling damaged books and turning the print and pictures into rose rings

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Nick Hewer and Rachel Riley present the words-and-numbers game, with Richard Madeley joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Diane and Sam try to help a dejected Frasier rebuild his self-confidence by giving him an intriguing relationship to study - their own. Bar-room comedy, starring Ted Danson

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: In a strange reversal of their usual attitude, Diane and Carla compete to be Cliff's date for the postman's ball. Bar-room comedy, starring Ted Danson, Shelley Long and Rhea Perlman

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray's relatives reluctantly tell him the brutal truth about his performance as a panellist on Roy Firestone's live TV sports show. American comedy, starring Ray Romano

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray attends classes to learn a range of parenting skills, hoping they will help him be a better father to Ally and the twins - but ends up using them to keep Frank and Marie in line. Ray Romano stars

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray reluctantly agrees to take Robert out on the town - but makes the mistake of revealing that the evening was not his idea, causing relations between them to nosedive

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The prospect of Christmas with his son Frederick leaves Frasier panicking about what presents to buy him, and it takes a reminder from Martin about the festive spirit to keep him on the right track

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The psychiatrist realises the true depth of his feelings for station manager Kate, only for her to announce she is moving to Chicago. Kelsey Grammer and Mercedes Ruehl star

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The psychiatrist suddenly realises he has a shortage of friends in Seattle, as most of his old ones are back in Boston. He announces on air he is looking for companionship and gets a lot more than he bargained for. Guest starring Griffin Dunne, with Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim and Margreeth insulate a gîte using their flock of 11 sheep, in a bid to keep the cold at bay for their guests. In the hope of being able to host her family at Christmas, Julia renovates the dining room, although removing render off the original chateau walls proves to be a problem. Plus, three years after bonding over their love of France, Manchester couple Joshua and Lee are on the hunt for the perfect chateau to call home

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Includes headlines and weather

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Lisa and Jesue begin the contest in this episode from Cambridge and Peterborough with an international menu. A tough crusted tart doesn't impress bickering partners Paul and Gareth and the presentation is also lacking for posh millennials Georgie and Alex. On day two Georgie and Alex cook up recipes devised by Alex's mother. Finally, Paul and Gareth plan an Italian menu. Using crystals and visualisation techniques, they endeavour to serve their duck breast medium rare and hope that their mousse is fluffy. However, things fail to go to plan

programme.title: Steph's Packed Lunch
programme.desc: Steph McGovern presents the weekday magazine, including celebrity guests, food ideas, real-life features and entertainment, lifestyle and consumer stories. Plus, a sideways look at the news in One O'Clock Views

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Nick Hewer and Rachel Riley present the long-running words-and-numbers game, with guest Janet Street-Porter alongside Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: The Answer Trap
programme.desc: Bolton friends Neil and David take on married couple Ravi and Shilla from Birmingham, with both teams hoping to steer clear of the traps and play for £10,000 in the final round. Quiz, hosted by Anita Rani

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: After enjoying many holidays together on Tenerife, Terry and Jen wish to celebrate their recent engagement by buying their own holiday home on the south of the island. To help them fulfil their dream, Jonnie Irwin hunts out five options for their £150,000 budget for the pair to consider

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Brody and Rima Jamison welcome their rivals to the Cadillac Kustomz Hotel on the Isle of Bute in Scotland. Everything initially seems to be going to plan, with Liz and Dave stunned by the scenery, and Jill and Sharron revelling in the retro design. The B&Bers are in perfect harmony on a night of rock 'n' roll singing, but it all goes downhill in the diner in the morning when the Scottish-American breakfast is lost in translation

programme.title: Beat the Chef
programme.desc: Bride-to-be Yemi King has her heart set on the £10,000 to fund her big day. She's hoping that her pan-fried chicken with a white wine and lemon sauce will be a match made in heaven with the food jury, but chef Frederick Forster and his spiced duck breast have other ideas. Cookery challenge, hosted by Andi Oliver

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge becomes fed up of ferrying her kids around free of charge, so she joins the ranks of an online taxi service. Homer, meanwhile, inadvertently causes Moe to become bankrupt, so to help his friend out he gets another job as caretaker at the power plant

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Mercedes and Sylver's news leaves Romeo heartbroken. Meanwhile, Sienna and Summer try their best to keep the truth from Brody

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Documentary featuring one of the most emotional and heart-rending stories ever filmed - that of Noel Fitzpatrick's own dog, Keira. In September 2020, Noel's life was turned upside down when the 13-year-old border terrier was hit by a car right outside the practice. Having sustained a ruptured abdominal lining and urinary bladder, a crushed sacrum and pelvis and a dislocated hip, Noel's colleagues worked tirelessly to repair extensive internal damage. Suddenly, the surgeon renowned for finding solutions for even the most challenging injuries, had to steel himself for the most important surgery of his life

programme.title: 21 Day Body Turnaround with Michael Mosley
programme.desc: The journalist and doctor asks five volunteers to put their lives in his hands as he attempts to turn their health around in just 21 days. Initial testing reveals all of them to likely be at risk from debilitating conditions such as diabetes, high blood pressure, heart disease, chronic inflammation and stress. Most have the aerobic fitness age of someone at least 20 years older. In the first episode, Michael investigates their digestive health and starts them out on a new training plan in conjunction with GP and fitness trainer Dr Folusha Oluwajana

programme.title: We Are Lady Parts
programme.desc: The band accost Amina and make her an offer - if she auditions with them, they'll set her up on a date with Ayesha's hot brother, Ahsan. Comedy about a Muslim punk band, with Anjana Vasan, Juliette Motamed, Lucie Shorthouse and Sarah Kameela Impey

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, as cameras capture their instant reactions prompted by the week's biggest and best shows, including Tom Allen's Quizness, Mare of Easttown, Gregg Wallace's Big Weekend Away, Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again, Dung Dynasty, Running Wild with Bear Grylls, The Andrew Marr Show, and ITV News

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Self-titled alpha male Josh struts in brimming with confidence, keen to find his very own Kardashian. Will glamorous 25-year-old Yasmin match his high standards? After years out of the dating game, waxing entrepreneur Sherie has decided now is the time to find a man. She is paired with fellow northerner Richard - but his dating anecdotes could backfire. Others hoping to make an impression are Disney-loving Jamie, musician Zander and Shannon, who says she is a 'weirdo - in a good way'

programme.title: Tom Allen's Quizness
programme.desc: This edition of the comedy quiz sees Emma, Bernie, Gemma and Harv battle it out for a shot at the jackpot, facing tricky questions on anything from Christopher Columbus to Mr Tumnus. The twist is that if they want to win the £25,000, the quizzers will need to prove their brains by looking daft

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef comes to the aid of a French restaurant in the heart of Moorpark, California. The pompous, over-complicated cuisine is keeping the customers away and there is an imminent threat of bankruptcy. However, the owner is vigorously opposed to change, despite the failings of his current business model

programme.title: Thappad (2020)
programme.desc: A woman is shocked when her husband hits her at a house party in front of all their guests, and when he refuses to accept the significance of his action, she decides to take a stand against him. Indian drama, starring Taapsee Pannu, Pavail Gulati and Maya Sarao

programme.title: Unreported World
programme.desc: Violent hurricanes and severe drought have decimated once fertile areas of Central America, with the region's rural poor worst affected. Hunger and child malnourishment is widespread, prompting an exodus of migrants to the United States. Reporter Guillermo Galdos tracks the journey of Gonzalo, who desperate to give his family a better life, risks his own by employing a network of people smugglers to illegally enter the US. The film reveals how climate migrants have become a valuable commodity in a booming trade and in a region controlled by crime cartels, some are paying the ultimate price

programme.title: The £100k Drop
programme.desc: Davina McCall hosts as mother and daughter Jayne and Claire from Jersey, and John and Jim, brothers from London, take part in the quiz in which contestants can win up to £100,000

programme.desc: Kirstie helps young couple Ferne and Peter, transform their empty flat into a brilliant space for entertaining, with the help of some crafty upcycling

programme.desc: Jonathan and Paddington must get their Ranger badges, but Mr Curry is not happy with them

programme.desc: Daddy Pig gets a new synthesiser, so the family collect sounds for it from around the house while he is out at work

programme.desc: The team has a special clock, which makes a loud sound when it is time to play, eat or sleep, but not all the Bunnies follow the routine

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Dermot Bannon helps Pat and Marita Harrold to renovate and extend the 1950s three-bedroom bungalow where Pat grew up and in which his mother still lives. The couple have moved house 17 times in the past 22 years, and hope to accommodate three generations of their family in the tiny, dark property. The architect plans to convert the original dwelling into a six-bed dormitory block with a new extension block offering living, dining and communal space - but the budget is under pressure and the clients express displeasure with the design

programme.title: 5 News at Lunchtime
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: Build a New Life in the Country
programme.desc: George Clarke meets Frank and Alice Freestone from Cambridge, who have decided to move to rural Croatia to convert a dilapidated stone house and its idyllic surroundings into a nature retreat with six log cabins for guests. While things seem to go well at first, difficulties with the local authorities, the language barrier and a divining rod cause problems for the project

programme.title: Home and Away

programme.desc: A teenager with a promising soccer career ahead of her moves to a new school, and finds herself playing alongside girls who were once her bitterest rivals. She finds herself subjected to a campaign of harassment by the coach and her daughter, and must find a way to prove her story before her academic and sporting futures are destroyed. Thriller, starring Christina Cox and Sarah Grey

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Welsh couple Ben and Kate hope to achieve their dream of running a bar in the sun. They have just £30,000 to fund their entire move and are checking out five bars to rent. But will any of them fit the bill? Jo and Jan hold an auction to raise funds for their dog rescue shelter, and car salesman Duncan comes up with a radical plan to help his Costa del Sol dealership stay afloat

programme.desc: The DIY expert helps homeowners transform the most important rooms in the house, and proves that not even a global pandemic can come between Brits and their beloved home makeovers. This time, Mark helps first-time buyer Maria in south-east London create a minimalist kitchen, enhanced with touches of natural wood and a wood-covered wall, for £5,000

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: Matt performs delicate eye surgery on a peacock in a bird lover's back garden, and Julian heads to a sheep farm where one of the rams needs a vasectomy. Meanwhile, Peter heads to see a worried farmer whose newborn calf is unable to stand up

programme.title: Our Yorkshire Farm
programme.desc: It's Christmas at Ravenseat Farm, and all nine children are home for the holidays, and this year, the family are busier than ever with a lot of calves to deliver. Fourteen-year-old Miles and his younger brother Sidney are asked to relocate their flock of hens, and work is piling up in the cowshed as Amanda's prize heifer Lily May is due to give birth to twin calves

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben meets a Glaswegian retired teacher who relocated to the mountains of Ethiopia, and leads an active life in her new home as she approaches her 70th birthday. She has made significant contributions to the local community, including setting up a school and building a restaurant on an isolated mountain top over the course of three years

programme.title: The Sex Business
programme.desc: Return of the documentary looking at how and why people buy, sell and market sex. The first episodes delves into the hidden world of female submissives, following four sex workers who reveal what life is really like as they try to make a living on the edges of the sex industry

programme.desc: Everyday life in a tattoo parlour catering to British tourists on Greece's Kavos strip. programme.title: Gregg Wallace's Big Weekend Away
programme.desc: The MasterChef judge is in Istanbul, exploring the history of a city that has been the centre of three empires. He tours the street food stands on the Asian side of the Bosphorus, learns the centuries-old techniques of the baklava makers, joins a kebab master at his grill, and eats dishes that would have graced the tables in the Ottoman palaces of old. Gregg finishes his weekend off with a special trip to an island just off the coast for a feast of traditional Turkish mezze

programme.title: Inside Greggs: Britain's Best Bakery
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the secrets of the successful bakery chain, offering a behind the scenes look at the products they sell and staff who work in their stores across the country. From the staff's secret pastry codes to the untold story behind Greggs' most famous recent success - the vegan sausage roll - the programme gives viewers a chance to learn more about one of Britain's most loved bakeries

programme.title: Broadmoor: Serial Killers & High Security
programme.desc: A rare insight into the workings of the high-security psychiatric hospital, Broadmoor, with testimony from those who have come face to face with Britain's most notorious killers. Broadmoor Hospital is home to around 200 of Britain's most dangerous men, and this documentary offers a glimpse of what life is like behind bars for some of its most famous inhabitants, including Peter Sutcliffe and Robert Maudsley

programme.title: Inside the Force: 24/7
programme.desc: When a woman allegedly threatens her neighbour with a knife, it results in a dramatic arrest with four police officers being assaulted, while Sergeant Mike arrests a volatile suspect who threatens hostel staff with a hammer

programme.title: 999: Criminals Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Footage of offences in the UK recorded on CCTV, revealing how it is being used by police forces to solve major crimes across Britain. The programme shows how CCTV helped detectives track down three men wanted for attempted murder outside a nightclub, and how it recorded a brazen cash machine gang who used explosives to conduct a reign of terror

programme.desc: In the Arctic Circle, the explorer meets Randi, the first Norwegian woman to climb Everest ahead of his own bid to scale the world's highest mountain. He embraces the tough and basic life on her 8.5 acre island by sleeping outdoors, and collecting raw building materials. Ben also experiences sea kayaking, and witnesses the northern lights

programme.title: Get Your Tatts Out: Kavos Ink
programme.desc: Three lads hit a Greek tattoo studio after a heavy night's drinking, only for one of them to wake up the next day with a 'burger nipple'. Another holiday-maker loses a bet which means he has to get a picture of his friends etched on his backside, and a Manchester bloke waltzes out of a tattoo studio with a pair of lips inked onto his bum

programme.title: Wildlife SOS

programme.title: Great Scientists
programme.desc: Historian Allan Chapman discusses Isaac Newton's revolutionary theories, which postulated that the universe is governed by a single set of mathematical principles. Newton produced a complex formula to describe the laws of gravity, and by the time of his death in 1727, the groundwork of modern physics had been laid

programme.title: Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Architect Dermot Bannon returns to offer more members of the public help with innovative design projects that should revolutionise their homes without calling for them to move. He begins in Puckaun, Co Tipperary, where teacher Darragh Egan, his partner Sarah Lovett and his 70-year-old mother Mary are seeking to convert their shared 1960s bungalow into two, self-contained wings. One will be for Mary, the other for Darragh, Sarah and their infant son Donagh. However, demolition delays caused by the existing building's foot-thick floors and walls leave local contractor Brendan Cormican and quality surveyor Patricia Power struggling to square Dermot's vision with the 200,000-euro budget

programme.title: Build a New Life in the Country
programme.desc: George Clarke revisits Paul and Tracy Fitzpatrick to see whether they have finally achieved their dream of a tranquil life in the Cambridgeshire countryside with their children. The couple's initial plans to convert a large apple store into a four-bedroom house in just three months was met with scepticism, and they soon discovered their property needed more work than anticipated

programme.desc: A newly engaged couple become intrigued by the young stranger who moves in next door. He is handsome, friendly and very helpful. What they don't realise is, he wants to be more than just a friend - and will do whatever it takes to achieve his goal. Thriller, starring Michael Welch, Haylie Duff, Tyler Johnson and Brittany Underwood

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Jan and Jose's animal sanctuary takes in a dog left with a badly injured back leg following a botched operation, while Bambi welcomes some new arrivals to his caravan park. Lawrence plans to give away free shots every time England score in a football match - but while he deals with a broken dishwasher, he also discovers that England are scoring far more than expected

programme.title: Home and Away

programme.title: Gardening with Carol Klein
programme.desc: Carol catches up with a few of the projects she started at the beginning of the season. There's a progress report on the new cut flower garden, the rapidly sprouting potatoes and the unruly beans and peas which are now ready to be tied in before their fruits begin to weigh heavy

programme.title: Kew Gardens: A Year In Bloom
programme.desc: It's summer and Kew Gardens is enduring a drought, which means the staff have plenty of watering to do, along with the regular weeding and maintenance. Work continues in the Tropical Nursery too as Sam tends to the orchid collection and Lara busies herself pollinating plants

programme.title: Escape to the Farm with Kate Humble
programme.desc: The presenter is looking forward to warmer days and celebrates by kayaking down the River Wye with her friend and expert Graham Symmonds. Along the way, they check out the wildlife, beautiful disused bridges and some unusual glacial boulders. Back in the kitchen, Kate rustles up Welsh cakes, cooked on the hot plate

programme.title: Chronic Pain: How to Live With It
programme.desc: Around 43 per cent of the UK population, roughly 28 million people, suffer from chronic pain conditions, and these symptoms can greatly affect quality of life. This observational documentary follows three people who face this painful reality on a daily basis as they undergo new treatments they've never tried before

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: After being run over by a car on her birthday, 73-year-old Sue arrives by ambulance with spinal and head injuries, and Dr Bijou and his team have to act quickly to save her life. Advanced clinical practitioner Leigh is looking after 17-year-old Ethan after accident at a skatepark, but she has concerns when he starts to show severe signs of memory loss

programme.title: Extreme Nightmare Tenants
programme.desc: Case of landlords with nightmare tenants and the difficulties they had in evicting them, including Yossi Harel, who rented out his family home in north London to take a job in Israel. He thought he had the perfect tenants - a teacher and his family - but before long they stopped paying rent. Plus, a builder who came up against his tenant and her aggressive boyfriend and a lawyer tasked with evicting an anti-social tenant in Blackpool
